channel.title: Cake on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Cake on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Texas Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Sherry and Paul Beaulieu

Cousins of the Bride

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1 1/2 cups white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-19 oz can of crushed pineapple

Combine all ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch glass pan. Cook at 325 degrees for 45-55 minutes. Insert a toothpick in the centre of the cake to check if it’s ready.

Sauce:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2/3 cup Carnation milk
  • 1 cup coconut
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Combine margarine, brown sugar and milk in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Continue boiling sauce for 5 minutes and then add coconut and vanilla.

item.title: Stock Club Cake with Coffee Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Dean Pigeau and Karen Cosgrove

Cousin of the Bride

Cake:

  • 1 1/2 cup flour
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 1/2 tsp soda
  • 1/4 cup cocoa
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 7/8 cup water

Bake for 1 hour or less at 350 degrees.

Coffee Icing:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 tsp coffee
  • 1 tbsp margarine
  • 2 tsp boiling water
  • 1 tsp cocoa
  • 1 cup icing sugar

Instructions

Mix coffee, margarine, water and cocoa until smooth. Slowly add icing sugar until creamy.

item.title: Sour Cream Coffee Cake with Strueusel
item.description:
Submitted by Christine and Graham Baker

Cousins of the Bride

A rich sour cream coffee cake which may be layered or simply topped with tart cranberries and spicy sweet streusel. Wonderful served warm with ice cream or whipped cream.

Streusel:

  • ¾ cup lightly packed brown sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup all purpose flour
  • ¼ cup butter

Mix together sugar, flour, and cinnamon. Blend in butter until crumbly.

Cake:

Ingredients

  • ½ cup soft butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup dairy sour cream
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 cups cranberries, fresh or frozen

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time, then vanilla. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add to creamed mixture. Add sour cream.

item.title: Picnic Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Shirley and Rob Webster

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Cake:

  • ½ cup margarine
  • 2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 ¼ cup white sugar
  • 1 tbsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup peanut butter
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup milk
  • 2 eggs

Cream margarine and sugar. Add peanut butter, vanilla and eggs. Beat well. In a separate bowl, blend together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to creamed mixture, alternating with milk. Mix well after each addition.

item.title: Mix-In-The-Pan Carrot Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Jeannine and Elmer Bell

Parents of the Groom

  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 ½ cup shredded carrots
  • ½ cup oil
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • ¼ cup water
  • icing sugar, optional
  • ½ tsp each of salt, baking soda and baking powder

With fork, in an 8 or 9 inch square pan, mix flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.

item.title: Malva Pudding Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Claire and Marc Massicotte

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Cake:

  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tbsp apricot jam
  • 1 cup flour
  • l tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • generous pinch of salt
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp white vinegar
  • 1 cup milk

Beat the egg and sugar well with a mixer. In a separate bowl, combine the jam, sifted flour, soda, baking powder and salt. In another bowl, melt butter and add the vinegar. Add liquids to egg mixture alternately with the flour.

item.title: Ice Box Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Lorna and Zoran Popovitch

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 30 marshmallows, chopped fine
  • 30 graham wafers, rolled fine
  • 18 cherries, chopped
  • ½ cup walnuts, chopped
  • 1 can Eaglebrand Milk
  • 1 cup coconut

Instructions

Mix the first four ingredients with the can of Eaglebrand. Butter a shallow pan and spread thick with half the coconut.

Spread the Eaglebrand mixture over the coconut, then spread another layer of coconut on top. Pack well and put in the fridge for 24 hours.

item.title: Granny Sayer's Rum Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Pat and Tom Sayer

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

  • 1 yellow Duncan Hines cake mix
  • 1 small instant Jello vanilla pudding
  • 4 eggs
  • ½ cup Crisco oil
  • ½ cup water
  • ¼ cup White Rum

Mix above ingredients together and bake in a greased bundt pan to which ¼ cup of chopped pecans has been added. Bake at 350 degrees for 55 minutes.

Topping:

In a sauce pan, combine the following:

item.title: Graham Coconut Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Margaret and Walter Smith

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter, room temperature
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup medium coconut
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • pinch of salt

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar together. Add beaten egg. Add other ingredients and mix well.

Place in a 9x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Serve with ice cream or whipped cream.

item.title: Drumstick Cake
item.description:
Submitted by Kevin Roy

Cousin of the Bride

Base:

  • 1 ½ cups vanilla or graham crumbs
  • ½ cup chopped peanuts or walnuts
  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 2 tbsp peanut butter

Mix the above ingredients and place in a 9x13 inch pan. Save some of the mixture for the topping.

Filling:

Ingredients

  • 250 gr cream cheese
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • ½ cup peanut butter
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 4 eggs, add one at a time
  • 1 large container Cool Whip

Instructions

Mix the above ingredients and layer on top of the base.

item.title: Cherry Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Stella and Ben Daigle

Friends of the Bride’s Family

Ingredients

  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • ½ cup melted butter
  • 2 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1-8 oz pkg of Philadelphia cream cheese
  • 1 large pkg of Dream Whip
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 can cherry pie filling

Instructions

Mix graham crumbs, melted butter and brown sugar together. Press into an 8x12 inch pan.

Mix Dream Whip according to directions on package. Add cream cheese and white sugar to whipped Dream Whip. Spread over graham crumbs. Top with cherry pie filling.

item.title: Caramel Pecan Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Sandra Stewart

Friend of the Bride and Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 ready-to-use Graham pie crust
  • 20 caramel squares
  • 3 tbsp milk
  • ½ cup chopped pecans
  • 2 pkgs cream cheese, softened
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 eggs

Instructions

Microwave caramels and milk until melted (about 2 min; stir after 1 min). Stir in pecans and mix well. Pour into pie crust and chill for 10 minutes.

Beat (medium speed) softened cream cheese, sugar and eggs until smooth. Pour into chilled pie crust.

item.title: Blueberry Cheesecake
item.description:
Submitted by Joanne and Greg Craig

Cousins of the Bride

Crust:

  • ½ cup butter
  • 2 cups graham crumbs
  • ¼ cup sugar

Topping:

  • 2 pkgs Dream Whip
  • 1 pkg cream cheese
  • ½ cup sugar

Filling:

Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • ¼ cup water
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 4 cups fresh/frozen blueberries

Note: Above filling can be substituted for 2 cans of blueberry pie filling.

item.title: Birthday Cake with German Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Louise and Dan Pigeau

Aunt and Uncle of the Bride

Cake:

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 1/4 cup sugar
  • dash of salt
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 2 eggs
  • 3/4 cup oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Mix first four ingredients together, then add remaining ingredients. Bake at 350 degrees for 30-45 minutes

German Icing:

Ingredients

  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2/3 cup brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup can milk
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1/4 tsp salt

Instructions

Boil until it starts to thicken and add 1 ½ cup of coconut and ½ cup of nuts.

item.title: Banana Cake with Caramel Icing
item.description:
Submitted by Shirley and Dave Jodouin

Cousins of the Groom

Cake:

  • 2 ½ cups flour
  • 1 ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 ½ tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 ½ cup white sugar
  • ¼ tsp cinnamon
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup mashed bananas
  • 1 cup buttermilkor1 cup sourmilk (add 2 tbsp vinegar to your regular cup of milk)

Throw all the ingredients into a big bowl and mix. Pour into a 3 L pan that has been greased, and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes or until a knife inserted in the middle comes out clean and dry.

item.title: Far East Crab Cakes
item.description:
If you’d like to serve this as an appetizer, divide the crab mixture evenly into 16 small patties, These smaller versions will have about 31 calories each.

Ingredients

  • 2 (6oz) cans lump crabmeat, drained
  • 1 tsp pepper
  • ½ cup finely chopped water chestnuts
  • 1 tsp dark sesame oil
  • ¼ cup soft breadcrumbs
  • 3 Tbsp reduced-calorie margarine
  • 1 Tbsp plus 1 tsp dried parsley flakes
  • Vegetable cooking spray

Instructions

Combine first 7 ingredients in a small bowl; stir well. Shape into 8 patties. Coat a large. Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; place over medium-high heat until hot. Add crabmeat patties and cook 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly browned on both sides, turning once.

item.title: White Christmas Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter
  • 5 eggs
  • 1-1/4 cup sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 cup red glazed cherries
  • 1 cup green glazed cherries
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time. Add flour. Mix well. Add cherries and nuts. Mix well. Bake in greased angelfood tin or a pan lined with well-greased brown paper.

Bake for 1-1/2 – 2 hours at 300 degrees.

item.title: Tomato Soup Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp nutmeg
  • ½ tsp cloves
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 1 cup raisins or dates

Instructions

Cream together shortening and sugar. Fold in beaten egg. Add soup. Mix well. Stir in flour, spices, salt, baking soda, sifted together. Add raisins and nuts last. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Ice with cream cheese frosting if desired.

item.title: Super Moist Chocolate Mayo Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 box chocolate cake mix
  • 1 cup Hellmann’s Mayonnaise
  • 1 cup water
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp ground cinnamon (optional)

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.

Blend all ingredients.

Bake as directed on the box.

item.title: Pineapple Cupcakes
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup shortening
  • ½ cup white sugar
  • ½ cup light brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-3/4 cup flour
  • ½ tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup crushed pineapple including some syrup
  • ½ cup water

Instructions

Into beater bowl measure shortening and sugars and beat until creamy. Beat in eggs. Remove beaters.

Sift together flour, baking powder and salt. Combine pineapple, water and vanilla. Stir flour mixture into creamed mixture alternately with the pineapple-water mixture until blended. Line the muffin pans with pleated paper cups and carefully spoon in batter to fill 7/8 full. Bake at 350 degrees 20 – 25 minutes. Cool in paper cups on cake rack.

item.title: Rhubarb Upside-Down Cake
item.description:
• 3 cups sliced fresh rhubarb, ½” pieces
  • 1 Tbsp unsalted butter, room temperature
  • ¼ cup strawberry or seedless raspberry jam
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1-1/2 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 2/3 cup butter, room temperature
  • 2 eggs
  • ¼ tsp each vanilla & almond extract
  • 1/3 cup milk

Put rhubarb in a medium bowl. Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Generously coat a deep dish pie plate with margarine.

item.title: Nugget 1942 Johnny Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup yellow cornmeal
  • 1 cup stirred but unsifted cake flour
  • 5 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • 2 Tbsp corn or other cooking oil
  • 1/3 cup corn syrup
  • 1 Tbsp butter or drippings

Instructions

Turn the oven to 425 degrees. Next put the sifter over a bowl and into it measure the cornmeal, flour, baking powder and salt. Sift into bowl. At this stage measure the butter or drippings into a 9” x 9” pan and put it in the oven to heat up to sizzling point. To dry ingredients add egg, milk, oil and corn syrup and stir just enough to mix. Carefully remove hot pan from oven and pour batter into sizzling hot fat. Return to oven at 425 degrees and bake 20 minutes.

item.title: Nancy Lemon Coffee Cake
item.description:
• 1 large lemon or any flavour cake mix
  • 1 3-3/4 pkg Jello Lemon Instant pudding
  • 1 cup water
  • ½ cup oil
  • 4 eggs

Mix all together.

TOPPING;

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts

Instructions

Grease tube pan. Sprinkle half topping mixture. Pour half of cake mixture. Add rest of topping mixture. Pour rest of batter on top.

Bake in a 350 degree oven for 50 minutes. (I use a large angel food cake tin.)

item.title: Mock Cheese Cake
item.description:
FILLING:

  • 1 14-oz fruit cocktail
  • 1 small carton sour cream
  • Sugar to taste 1 – 2 tsp.

Drain cocktail. Set out on paper towel to dry off. Mix with sour cream. Set aside.

MIX:

Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup graham wafer crumbs
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • ¼ cup melted butter

Instructions

Press in 8”x 8”or 6”x 11”pan. Bake until light brown. Cool. Pour filling on cool shell. Sprinkle remaining crumbs on top. Store in refrigerator several hours or overnight.

item.title: Mix-in-the-pan Carrot Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp nutmeg
  • ½ tsp each salt, baking soda, baking powder
  • 1-1/2 cups shredded carrots
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten
  • 1/2 cup oil
  • 1/4 cup water
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla
  • icing sugar, optional

Instructions

In 8”or 9”square pan with fork mix well flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking soda, baking powder and nutmeg. Add carrots, eggs, oil, water and vanilla.

Mix briskly with a fork until thoroughly blended. Bake in preheated 350 degree oven about 30 minutes or until pick inserted in center comes out clean and cake pulls away from sides of pan. Cool in pan on rack. Sprinkle with icing sugar or ice with cream cheese frosting.

item.title: Mexican Wedding Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups flour
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup walnuts
  • 2 eggs
  • 19-oz tin crushed pineapple

Mix and bake in a 9”x 9” greased pan in a preheated 350 degree oven for 50 – 60 minutes.

Ice with cream cheese frosting:

Ingredients

  • 8 oz cream cheese (250 gr.)
  • ¼ cup butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 lb icing sugar

item.title: Mirsh's Chocolate Zucchini Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • ¾ tsp nutmeg
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup cocoa
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 cups sugar
  • ½ cup oil
  • ¾ cup buttermilk OR 2 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 cups grated zucchini

Instructions

Mix all together. Bake in 350 degree F. oven for 40 – 60 minutes.

item.title: Hundred Dollar Cake
item.description:
First Step:

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 4 tbsp cocoa
  • 1-3/4 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • Pinch of salt

Second Step:

Ingredients

  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 cup cold water
  • 1 tsp vanilla (Beat well)

Instructions

Sift dry ingredients together. Add to liquid mixture and beat for 2 minutes. Bake in 9”x 9” cake pan for 40 minutes at 350 degrees. - 25 minutes for cupcakes.

item.title: Hot Milk Sponge Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 large eggs
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup milk
  • 3 tbsp margarine or butter
  • 1 cup all-purpose-flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder

Instructions

Into beater bowl break eggs and beat until fluffy then slowly add sugar until very light and doubled in bulk.  Beat in salt & vanilla.  In a pan heat together the milk and margarine OR butter to just below boiling point.  While very hot pour into egg mixture BEATING ALL THE TIME.  Remove beaters.

item.title: Gumdrop Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups pastry flour (1-3/4 cup all-purpose)
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 lb cooking gumdrops
  • 1 lb seedless raisins

Instructions

Cream sugar and butter; add eggs. In separate bowl, dredge gumdrops and raisins with ½ cup of the flour. To the creamed mixture, add 1 cup flour and ½ cup milk, beating well after each addition. Add remaining flour with baking powder and salt. Beat well. Add remaining milk and the floured raisins and gumdrops. Mix thoroughly. Bake in a well-greased loaf pan in a 350 degree oven for 1 hour.

item.title: German Fruit Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1/3 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 tbsp milk
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • Pinch of salt

Instructions

Cream butter and add sugar little by little, then add the eggs and salt. Sift flour with baking powder. Add to egg and sugar mixture alternately with milk. Beat well. Pour into a well-greased and floured flan pan. Bake 15 minutes in a 325 degree oven. Knock firmly on bottom of pan when you take it out of the oven to loosen it from pan. Cool. Invert on a place and fill it with fruit or filling of your choice. Top with whipped cream if desired.

item.title: Fruit Cocktail Cake
item.description:
300 degrees - 50 – 60 minutes

Ingredients for Cake:

  • 2 eggs
  • 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp Baking Soda
  • 1 – 14 oz tin fruit cocktail (drained but reserve juice) – Slightly crush fruit w/a fork

Ingredients for Topping:

Ingredients

  • 1-3/4 cup W. Sugar
  • ½ cup pure Carnation Milk
  • ½ cup butter or margarine

Instructions

Directions for Cake: Beat eggs with Sugar. Put juice & Baking Soda in eggs & beat. Add Fruit Cocktail & beat. Add flour & beat. Pour into a 13” x 15” well-greased pan. Bake for 50 - 60 minutes in a 300 degree oven. Test w/a toothpick for doneness.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Carrot Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups white sugar
  • 4 eggs
  • 1-1/2 cups oil
  • 2 Tbsp cinnamon
  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 Tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 3 cups grated carrots
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Blend sugar and oil. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each one. Beat well. Sift together dry ingredients. Add gradually to first mixture. Add carrots and nuts last.

Bake in 9” x 15” pan 45 minutes approx. at 350 F.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Triple Fudge Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 package chocolate pudding mix (cooked type)
  • 1 package chocolate or devil’s food cake mix
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Prepare chocolate pudding mix according to package directions. Blend dry chocolate or devil’s food cake mix into hot pudding.

Pour into greased and floured 13" x 9" pan.

Sprinkle with chocolate chips. Bake at 350°F for 30–35 minutes.

item.title: Edna's Lemon Cake
item.description:
All who attended Creation Unique’s Camp Wanna-sew enjoyed Edna’s Lemon Cake.

  • 1 Lemon Cake Mix
  • 1 Jello Instant Pudding Mix

Add:

Ingredients

  • 4 eggs
  • 1 cup of water
  • ½ cup oil

Instructions

Mix well. Pour into pan and bake for 1 hour at 325 degrees.

For the glaze, Edna stirred lemon juice with lemon juice.

item.title: Double Strawberry Sheet Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg strawberry cake mix
  • 1 pkg strawberry Jello (4 servings)

Instructions

Dissolve Jello in ¾ cup boiling water. Add ½ cup cold water; set aside. Mix and bake cake as directed in a 9”x 13” pan. Cool 20 – 25 minutes. Poke deep holes about 1”apart in cake (still in pan) with a meat fork or large nail.

Pour Jello into holes.

Refrigerate cake while preparing Quick and Easy Icing made with whipped topping and instant pudding.

item.title: Carrot Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup oil
  • 2 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 4 eggs, beaten
  • 3 cups grated carrots
  • 1 cup finely chopped walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Mix all together. Do not overbeat. Bake for 1 hour at 350 F. Check after 35 minutes. Do not overbake.

Use a greased angelfood cake pan or a 9 x 13 pan.

item.title: Cherry Walnut Fruitcake
item.description:
This fruitcake is an easy- to-make classic holiday offering. If you like, make it ahead, wrap and freeze for entertaining and gift-giving.

Ingredients

  • 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 eggs, lightly beaten
  • 1 jar (750 ml) mincemeat
  • 1 tin (300 ml) sweetened condensed milk
  • 2 cups chopped mixed glace fruit
  • 1 cup chopped walnuts
  • Whole glace cherries

Instructions

Grease two 9 x 5 loaf pans – set aside.

item.title: Cherry Cheese Cake
item.description:
• 1/2 to 1 cup icing sugar
  • 250 gr. Pkg cream cheese
  • 1 envelope Dream Whip or 1 cup whipping cream
  • 1 tin cherry pie filling
  • 1 graham crumb crust

Bake crumb crust at 350 degrees for 10 minutes. Cream cheese and sugar till nice and soft. Make Dream Whip and combine with cheese. Cover graham crust. Put cherries on top. Leave in refrigerator for 24 hours.

CRUST;

Ingredients

  • ¼ cup melted butter
  • 4 tbsp white sugar
  • 1-1/4 cup graham crumbs

Instructions

Combine and press in 9”x 9” pan.

item.title: Carrot Cake Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 medium-sized carrots, peeled & grated
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ cup raisins
  • ¾ tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup healthy margarine
  • ¼ tsp each ground ginger, nutmeg, allspice
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup dark brown sugar
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 1 egg
  • 2-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ cup buttermilk
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

  1.  Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Coat 12 muffin cups with a little margarine. Measure 1 cup grated carrot into a bowl. Add raisins. Melt ½ cup margarine in microwave or saucepan. Let cool to room temperature.

  2.  Place flours, then baking powder, soda, salt and spices into large bowl. Add sugar and stir with a fork until evenly blended. Make a well in center. Place egg, buttermilk and vanilla in another bowl. Whisk with a fork. Whisk in cooled margarine. Pour into well. Stir just until dry ingredients are moistened. Add carrots and raisins. Stir until evenly distributed.

  3.  Immediately spoon into muffin cups. Do not press down.

  4.  Bake in center of oven until a skewer inserted in center of muffin comes out almost clean, 17 to 20 minutes. Cool on a rack. Makes 12 muffins.

1 muffin – 235 Calories, 4 gr Proteins, 9 gr Fat, 36 gr Carbohydrate

item.title: Bisquick Strawberry Shortcake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2-1/3 cups Bisquick
  • 3 Tbsp melted butter
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1 quart strawberries, sliced and sweetened
  • 3 Tbsp sugar

Instructions

Heat oven to 425 F. Stir all ingredients except strawberries until soft fough forms.

Drop by 6 spoonfuls onto ungreased baking sheet.

Bake 10 – 12 minutes. Split; fill and top with strawberries and whipped cream, if desired.

item.title: Purity Pancakes
item.description:
Blend together:

  • 1-1/2 cups flour
  • 1 – 2 tbsp sugar
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 1/8 tsp salt

Combine:

Ingredients

  • 1 beaten egg
  • 1-3/4 cup milk
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil or melted shortening

Instructions

Add liquids to the dry ingredients. Beat only until combined. Bake in a hot skillet slightly greased until edge of pancake begins to dry and lose its gloss. Turn and bake until golden brown.

NOTE: If batter thickens, add a little more milk.

item.title: Johnny Cake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour
  • 2 Tbsp sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 cup cornmeal
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 Tbsp melted butter

Instructions

Mix and sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Add cornmeal. Beat egg and milk and add to the flour mixture. Mix thoroughly and add melted butter.

Bake in a greased cake pan 8-inch square at 350 F. for 30 minutes.

item.title: Apple Dabble Cake
item.description:
• 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup vegetable oil
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 4 cups raw apples, chopped

Mix ingredients by hand in order given. Pour into 13x9 inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 to 40 minutes.

Combine 1 cup of brown sugar, 1/4 cup milk, 1/4 cup butter. Boil for 4 minutes. Pour over baked cake and return to oven for a few minutes. This is scrumptious!

item.title: Fruit Protein Pancake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 bananas
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 Tbps rolled oats
  • Olive oil

Instructions

  1. Whisk both eggs in a bowl.
  2. Mash up just 1 of the 3 bananas on a plate, using a fork.  Add to eggs and mix.
  3. Add the rolled oats to the egg/banana mix as well.
  4. Cut up the other 2 bananas in slices.
  5. In a pan (that you can cover), drizzle some olive oil to make sure the bottom is barely covered.
  6. Lay down all of the banana slices evenly around the pan, then pour the egg mixture over them.  Even it out with a spoon or your spatula.
  7. Cover and cook on low for 10 mins.
  8. Flip it over and cook on low for another 5 mins (still covered).  Pro-tip: Saw a neat trick to hold a plate upside down over your pan, tightly, then flip the pan and plate right over - to flip your pancake.  Worked like a charm!
  9. Cut it up and put some syrup or whipped cream or whatever you want to jazz it up!

Weight Watchers Points: 1 point for 1/3 of the pancake.

item.title: Carrot Cake Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 1/3 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 4 large carrots
  • 3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 tbsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter, white sugar, and brown sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Shred, pat dry, and then mix carrots in.
  6. Fold in flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
  7. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  8. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  9. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  10. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  11. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Confetti Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 and a 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 and a 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 2 and 3/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/4 cup sprinkles

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter and sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Fold in flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Mix in sprinkles.
  6. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  7. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  8. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  9. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  10. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Strawberry Cheesecake Keto Fat Bombs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup = 120 ml = 2.5 oz = 70 g strawberries, fresh or frozen
  • 3/4 cup = 180 ml = 5.3 oz = 150 g cream cheese, softened
  • 1/4 cup = 60 ml = 2 oz = 60 gbutterorcoconut oil, softened
  • 2 tablespoons = 0.7 oz = 20 gpowdered erythritolOR 10–15 dropsliquid stevia
  • 1 vanilla bean OR 1/2–1 tablespoonvanilla extract

Instructions

  1. Place the cream cheese and butter (or coconut oil) cut into small pieces in to a mixing bowl. Leave at room temperature for 30–60 minutes until softened. Make sure the butter is softened, otherwise it will be difficult to mix and achieve a smooth texture. Do not microwave the butter – the mixture needs to stay firm.
  2. Meanwhile, wash the strawberries and remove the green parts. Place them into a bowl and mash using a fork or place in a blender for a smooth texture.
  3. Add the powdered erythritol (or stevia), vanilla extract and mix well. Before you mix the strawberries with the remaining ingredients, make sure they have reached room temperature.
  4. Add to the bowl with softened butter and cream cheese.
  5. Use a hand whisk or food processor and mix until well combined.
  6. Spoon the mixture into small muffin silicon molds or candy molds. Place in the freezer for about 2 hours or until set.
  7. When done, unmold the fat bombs and place into a container. Keep in the freezer and enjoy any time!

Billy’s Notes:We used salted butter and they almost seemed a bit too salty instead of cheesecakey. Maybe used unsalted butter next time. We also used raspberries instead of strawberries, which may have made them tart instead of sweet.

item.title: Carrot Cake Oatmeal
item.description:
INGREDIENTS:

  • 1 tablespoon butter
  • 1 cup steel cut oats
  • 4 cups water
  • 1 cup grated carrots
  • 3 tablespoons maple syrup
  • 2 teaspoons cinnamon
  • 1 teaspoon pumpkin pie spice
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 3/4 cup raisins
  • 1/4 cup chia seeds

DIRECTIONS:

Add butter to pressure cooking pot, select Sauté. When butter is melted add the oats and toast, stirring constantly, until they smell nutty, about 3 minutes.

Add water, carrots, maple syrup, cinnamon, pumpkin pie spice, and salt. Select high pressure and set 10 minutes cook time.

item.title: Rhubarb Strawberry Cake (Tracey)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 3 cups sliced strawberries
  • 3 cups diced rhubarb
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup margarine
  • 1 cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
  2. In a large bowl, mix white sugar, 3 tbsp flour, strawberries, and rhubarb.
  3. Place mixture in a 9x13 pan.
  4. Mix 1 1/2 cups of flour, brown sugar, margarine, and oats till crumbly.
  5. Put crumble on top of rhubarb and strawberry mixture.
  6. Bake 45 mins in oven or until crisp and brown.

item.title: Lemon Coffee Cake - Pep's Grandma
item.description:
Ingredients

Mix together all of the following:

  • 1 Lemon Cake Mix
  • 1 Pkg. Lemon Instant Pudding
  • 1 Cup of Water
  • 1/2 Cup of Oil
  • 4 Eggs

Topping:

  • 2 Tbsp of cinnamon
  • 1/2 Cup of Brown Sugar
  • 1/2 Cup of Chopped Walnuts (Optional) (Billy’s Note: They’re gross - don’t do it!)

Directions:

Instructions

  1. Grease 2 regular loaf pans.
  2. Sprinkle 1/2 of the topping mixture on bottom of the pans.
  3. Pour half of the cake mixture next.
  4. Add the rest of the topping mixture.
  5. Pour the rest of the batter on top.
  6. Bake in a 350 degree oven for 50 minutes.
